old_claim.note: Herbal Medicine Preparation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No Abydos herbal-wine dependency remains because Roman institutional infirmaries are not documented as requiring or descending from that specific Predynastic recipe.

source_shape.source_locator: The paper concerns chemical residues from Egyptian wine jars and does not describe Roman valetudinaria, hospital organization, or institutional care.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source dates one remedy preparation but provides no causal bridge to Roman hospital institutions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Medicinal plants were used in many settings, yet a care facility can exist without the exact fermentation process that now defines the prerequisite node.

ontology_before: General medicinal-plant history was used to connect an ancient recipe umbrella to Roman institutional care.
ontology_after: The infirmary node retains its military-care and infrastructure relationships without a specific remedy inheritance.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: public_hospitals_infirmaries no longer directly depends on herbal_medicine_preparation.
would_reject_if: A source demonstrated direct institutional adoption of the scoped Abydos herbal-wine process by Roman valetudinaria.
short_reason: The specific Abydos preparation does not enable the Roman hospital institution.
